perturbation.original_text: GLOBAL MASTER SUPPLY AGREEMENT This Global Master Supply Agreement ("Agreement" or "Contract") is between ExxonMobil Chemical Company, a division of Exxon Mobil Corporation ("Seller"),on behalf of itself and in the interest of the ExxonMobil affiliates listed on Attachment B (each an "ExxonMobil Selling Affiliate" or "EMCC/A" or collectively, "ExxonMobil Selling Affiliates"), and West Pharmaceutical Services, Inc. ("Buyer"), on behalf of itself and the Buyer affiliates listed on Attachment C (each a "Buyer Affiliate" or "West/A" or collectively, "Buyer Affiliates").
perturbation.changed_text: GLOBAL MASTER SUPPLY AGREEMENT This Global Master Supply Agreement ("Agreement" or "Document") is between ExxonMobil Chemical Company, a division of Exxon Mobil Corporation ("Vendor"),on behalf of itself and in the interest of the ExxonMobil affiliates listed on Attachment B (each an "ExxonMobil Selling Affiliate" or "EMCC/A" or collectively, "ExxonMobil Selling Affiliates"), and West Pharmaceutical Services, Inc. ("Purchaser"), on behalf of itself and the Buyer affiliates listed on Attachment C (each a "Buyer Affiliate" or "West/A" or collectively, "Buyer Affiliates").
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Contract' to 'Document', 'Seller' to 'Vendor', and 'Buyer' to 'Purchaser' creates confusion. While the new terms are introduced as potentially interchangeable with the original terms, it isn't explicitly stated they are the same throughout the entire document. This introduces ambiguity regarding which entity is bound by specific obligations later in the agreement. This contradicts the entire document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ORDER OF PRECEDENCE In the event of conflict between this Agreement (including the Failure in Performance and Change in Circumstances clauses herein) and the Attachments hereto, this Agreement shall prevail, except with respect to the governing law specified in Attachments A, G and H, respectively, and as applicable to the appropriate ExxonMobil Selling Affiliate.
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces different terms ('Vendor', 'Purchaser', 'Document') to refer to the same entities and agreement initially defined as 'Seller', 'Buyer', and 'Contract', respectively. While the modified text implies these terms might be interchangeable, it does not explicitly guarantee they will be used consistently throughout the entire document. This creates ambiguity, especially when the 'ORDER OF PRECEDENCE' clause prioritizes 'this Agreement'. If some sections refer to 'this Document' or 'Vendor' and 'Purchaser', it's unclear if the precedence clause applies to them in the same way, potentially leading to conflicting interpretations and creating an in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology'.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer Affiliates shall issue a purchase order(s), or call off order(s) when purchasing Product from ExxonMobil Selling Affiliates in writing pursuant to this Contract ("Purchase Order"). Such Purchase Order(s) shall specify (a) the quantity of Product, and (b) general date of delivery. All Purchase Orders agreed to be filled by an ExxonMobil Selling affiliate shall be deemed to be a separate agreement between the relevant ExxonMobil Selling Affiliate and the relevant Buyer Affiliate, incorporating the terms of this Contract. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in the attachments ExxonMobil and the ExxonMobil Selling Affiliates shall not unreasonably reject any Purchase Order that otherwise complies with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer Affiliates shall issue a purchase order(s), or call off order(s) when purchasing Product from ExxonMobil Selling Affiliates in writing pursuant to this Agreement ("Purchasing Document"). Such Purchase Order(s) shall specify (a) the quantity of Product, and (b) general date of delivery. All Purchase Orders agreed to be filled by an ExxonMobil Selling affiliate shall be deemed to be a separate agreement between the relevant ExxonMobil Selling Affiliate and the relevant Buyer Affiliate, incorporating the terms of this Document. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in the attachments ExxonMobil and the ExxonMobil Selling Affiliates shall not unreasonably reject any Purchase Order that otherwise complies with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Purchase Order' to 'Purchasing Document' within the context of fulfilling obligations under 'this Agreement' creates an in-text contradiction. The subsequent sentence refers to 'Purchase Orders' being agreed upon and incorporated into the 'this Document' again, linking 'Purchase Order' to 'this Document', but not the 'Agreement.' This makes the specific obligation of not unreasonably rejecting 'Purchase Orders' ambiguous as it references the 'Agreement' directly. 'Agreement', 'Contract' and 'Document' were already made interchangeable in the first perturbation, so there is ambiguity about what 'Agreement' refers to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Buyer Affiliates shall issue a purchase order(s), or call off order(s) when purchasing Product from ExxonMobil Selling Affiliates in writing pursuant to this Agreement ("Purchasing Document"). Such Purchase Order(s) shall specify (a) the quantity of Product, and (b) general date of delivery. All Purchase Orders agreed to be filled by an ExxonMobil Selling affiliate shall be deemed to be a separate agreement between the relevant ExxonMobil Selling Affiliate and the relevant Buyer Affiliate, incorporating the terms of this Document. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in the attachments ExxonMobil and the ExxonMobil Selling Affiliates shall not unreasonably reject any Purchase Order that otherwise complies with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Purchase Order' to 'Purchasing Document' in the initial sentence, while maintaining 'Purchase Order' in subsequent sentences, creates ambiguity regarding which document the obligation to not unreasonably reject applies to. The original text clearly links the obligation to 'this Agreement' via the Purchase Order, but the changed text inconsistently uses 'Purchasing Document' and 'Agreement' which creates a contradiction. The definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction states this '...uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.' Because this confusion arises within the same section, referencing the same obligation, and because 'Agreement', 'Contract' and 'Document' were made interchangeable, this constitutes a strong, in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree to explore the reuse/recycling of articles, manufacturing trim and scrap Buyer produces from Products purchased from Seller.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree to explore the reuse/recycling of articles, manufacturing trim and scrap Purchaser produces from Goods purchased from Vendor.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Products' to 'Goods', 'Seller' to 'Vendor' and 'Buyer' to 'Purchaser' changes the scope of the agreement. 'Products' was originally defined to include specific items whereas 'Goods' is not defined. The modified sentence suggests a broader scope, which is inconsistent with the rest of the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance with the provisions of this Agreement, ExxonMobil Selling Affiliates agree to sell to Buyer Affiliates, and Buyer Affiliates agree to purchase from ExxonMobil Selling Affiliates, the following product(s) (collectively, "Product"): Products Quantity [Metric Tons / Year] Container PackageYear 2019 2020 2021 2022 2023 [*****] [*****] Minimum Maximum [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] Leased metal crates [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] Leased metal crates [*****] not defined Leased metal crates Subject to this Agreement's terms and conditions, Buyer Affiliates shall purchase and ExxonMobil Selling Affiliates shall sell the yearly minimum amount of Product amounts (in the aggregate) listed above. Buyer or Buyer Affiliates may request to purchase amounts over the Product maximum amounts per year, however, it shall be solely within Seller or any ExxonMobil Selling Affiliate's discretion whether and under which conditions to accommodate Buyer's request.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified 'Products' purchased from 'Seller' and produced by 'Buyer', which is consistent with the 'Product' definition in the contradicted text referring to 'ExxonMobil Selling Affiliates' selling to 'Buyer Affiliates'. By changing 'Products' to 'Goods', 'Seller' to 'Vendor', and 'Buyer' to 'Purchaser', the modified text broadens the scope to potentially include items or parties not originally covered under the agreement. Since 'Goods' is undefined, and the buyer and seller terms have changed, the specific 'Product' as defined in the contradicted section is no longer guaranteed to be what is being discussed in the changed text, creating a direct contradiction. The changed text opens the door to ambiguity, which creates a contradiction with the original product definition. This is a misaligned terminology contradiction as defined above.
